page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 8 physical cores, operating with a single thread per core configuration. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This server configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. The absence of hyperthreading means each vCPU has exclusive access to a physical core, which prevents resource contention at the thread level but limits the total concurrent thread capacity compared to hyperthreaded alternatives. Additionally, the CPU does not expose advanced vector, matrix, or cryptographic instruction sets such as AVX-512 or AMX.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance is structured for memory-optimized workloads. This resource density allows for qualitative cost efficiency in scenarios requiring significant memory capacity without the overhead of additional compute cores. Appropriate workloads include in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that do not require local disk I/O or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. The hardware configuration provides a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 8 physical cores and a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated execution resources without hyperthreading contention. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, necessitating network-attached storage for data persistence. The lack of advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX limits its suitability for specialized vector math or machine learning acceleration. However, the high memory density per core offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications. It is designed for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated single-threaded cores and high RAM density for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with single-thread execution
64.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization on x86_64 architecture
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive processing
